meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't deploy apps. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Project Template: Solve Deployment Blocks Now
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment. Use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to launch your first AI app. A step-by-step guide for student builders.
content.answer_block: If you are stuck on deployment, the Antigravity template is your bridge from local code to a live, production-ready environment. By pairing this standardized boilerplate with Claude Code, you automate infrastructure, environment variables, and cloud configuration, turning a deployment nightmare into a single, seamless, and professional command-line execution.
content.intro:
You have spent hours crafting the perfect logic, debugging your AI agent, and refining the UI. But when it comes time to ship, the screen goes dark. Deployment errors, environment variable mismatches, and infrastructure confusion turn your hard-earned progress into a frustrating wall. You aren't failing as a developer; you are just missing the bridge between code and cloud.

The Antigravity project template is the industry-standard architecture designed specifically to eliminate these bottlenecks. It is the same foundation used by senior engineers to move from prototype to production in minutes. Today, we turn that wall into a door.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Workspace
steps.description: Start by cloning the boilerplate that handles all your CI/CD pipelines and environment secrets out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity production-ready template for a Next.js AI application.

steps.title: Configure Infrastructure-as-Code
steps.description: Automate the provisioning of your cloud environment so you never have to manually configure a server again.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the necessary Terraform scripts for a Vercel-ready environment with Supabase integration.

steps.title: Sync Environment Secrets
steps.description: Securely map your API keys using the Antigravity secrets manager to prevent deployment-time failures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the .env.production file based on my current local environment variables.

steps.title: Automated Build Validation
steps.description: Run a dry-run build check to identify dependency conflicts before pushing to your production branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run a pre-flight build check and identify any potential build-time errors in the current codebase.

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Execute the final push to your live URL using the automated deployment pipeline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initiate the deployment sequence to the main production branch and provide the live URL.

pro_tips: Always run a --dry-run flag on your first deployment to catch environment variable mismatches before they hit the cloud.
Use the 'antigravity-watch' mode to automatically trigger build checks every time you save a significant feature file.
Keep your secrets in a separate vault; never commit them, even if you think the repo is private.
If a build fails, ask Claude Code to 'diff the build logs against the last successful deployment' to find the exact line causing the regression.
Standardize your project structure early; the Antigravity template forces a clean separation of concerns that makes debugging infinitely easier.
content.next_steps: Once your first app is live, focus on setting up monitoring and logging tools like Sentry or LogRocket to track how real users interact with your AI agent.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the template, and the roadmap. Stop letting deployment anxiety hold your talent back. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
